meta.description: v1.0 structural evals (field presence, types, arrays non-empty) + v1.1 quality evals (perspective independence, domain grounding, synthesis quality, Socratic challenge, actionability). Quality evals use agent-as-judge with rubric at evals/quality-rubric.md.

test_cases.name: Search relevance metric choice (easy, with --domain)
test_cases.description: Clear single-topic question about offline metric selection for search. Domain file provides rich context (NDCG, MRR, QSR). All 3 personas should engage with domain-specific concepts.

input.question: Should we switch from NDCG@10 to MRR as our primary offline metric for Rovo search?

scope_check.reason: Single clear analysis question about metric selection

perspectives_present.check: All 3 perspective keys exist in output

perspective_fields.check: Each perspective has required observation contract fields

domain_references.check: Each persona references at least 1 domain-specific concept

required_fields.blind_spots: array (may be empty for narrow questions, but field must exist)

socratic_dialogue.check: At least 1 round of Socratic dialogue captured

required_fields.dialogue_history: array with length >= 2 (1 challenger + 1 user minimum)

socratic_dialogue.simulated_user_response: test case file contains ## Simulated User Response section — use it as the user's reply in the Socratic loop

json_parseable.check: Structured output JSON is valid and parseable

perspective_independence.rationale: Clean methodology question with natural persona divergence: Methodology Critic on metric validity, Stakeholder Advocate on user outcomes, Pragmatist on label availability and rebaselining cost.

domain_grounding.rationale: search-relevance domain file provides rich vocabulary (NDCG, MRR, QSR, BM25, cross-encoder, L1/L2, position bias, click logs). All personas should use domain terms in findings, not just domain_references arrays.

synthesis_quality.rationale: Methodology Critic likely wants debiasing and correlation analysis before switching; Pragmatist pushes for simpler side-by-side. Tension should surface but may not be as sharp as the churn case.

socratic_challenge.rationale: Should push on at least one unaddressed concern (e.g., rebaselining past experiments, offline-online correlation). Clean question means user may have good answers, so floor is 3.

actionability.rationale: next_actions should include: validate label types (graded vs binary), compute both metrics on existing eval sets, check offline-online metric correlation. Narrow question limits action plan scope.

test_cases.name: Churn prediction model (medium, no --domain)
test_cases.description: Common DS question without domain enrichment. The VP's 15% churn reduction target and quarterly timeline add stakeholder complexity. Tests that personas produce meaningful output without domain knowledge.

input.question: I want to build a churn prediction model. We have 2 years of user activity logs and payment data. The VP wants to reduce churn by 15% this quarter.

scope_check.reason: Single analysis question about churn prediction, despite timeline and stakeholder complexity

perspectives_present.check: All 3 perspective keys exist in output

perspective_fields.check: Each perspective has required observation contract fields

required_per_perspective.domain_references: array (may be empty — no domain loaded)

domain_references.check: domain_references array may be empty since no --domain flag

required_fields.blind_spots: array (may be empty for narrow questions, but field must exist)

socratic_dialogue.check: At least 1 round of Socratic dialogue captured

required_fields.dialogue_history: array with length >= 2 (1 challenger + 1 user minimum)

socratic_dialogue.simulated_user_response: test case file contains ## Simulated User Response section — use it as the user's reply in the Socratic loop

json_parseable.check: Structured output JSON is valid and parseable

perspective_independence.rationale: Strong natural divergence: Methodology Critic on prediction-intervention gap and causal inference, Stakeholder Advocate on VP's 15% intervention target, Pragmatist on quarterly timeline and 80/20 scoping.

domain_grounding.rationale: No --domain flag. Personas should use churn-specific DS vocabulary (causal inference, feature leakage, survivorship bias, intervention channels) but ceiling is 3 per rubric compressed scale.

synthesis_quality.rationale: This case has sharp built-in tensions: rigor vs timeline (causal analysis won't ship this quarter), prediction vs intervention (model doesn't reduce churn alone), 80/20 vs full pipeline. Synthesis must stage these as explicit exchanges.

socratic_challenge.rationale: Rich material for pushback: VP timeline vs methodological rigor, prediction vs intervention gap, intervention channel availability. Challenger should force user to choose.

actionability.rationale: VP's concrete 15% quarterly target creates strong actionability pressure. Findings should connect to decisions with IF/THEN structure and concrete timelines.

test_cases.name: Multi-topic scope check gate (hard, tests Phase 0 rejection)
test_cases.description: Input contains 3 distinct analysis topics. Phase 0 scope check should fire, reject the multi-topic input, and ask the user to pick one. No subagents should be dispatched.

input.question: I want to analyze our search ranking quality, build a recommendation engine, and also look at user segmentation for marketing.

scope_check.reason: Input contains 3 distinct analysis topics: (1) search ranking quality, (2) recommendation engine, (3) user segmentation for marketing
scope_check.expected_behavior: Agent surfaces the 3 topics and asks the user to pick one

no_subagent_dispatch.check: No subagent outputs should be produced

user_prompt.check: Agent asks user to select a single topic before proceeding
user_prompt.expected: Response should enumerate the detected topics and ask user to choose one

json_parseable.check: Not applicable — no structured output produced for scope-check rejections
